data.title: TI raises dividend by 4% to $1.42 per share, making it the 22nd year in a row, with an annualized total of $5.68.
data.description: Today, in Dallas, Texas Instruments Incorporated (TI) announced an increase in its quarterly cash dividend by 4%, up from $1.36 per share to $1.42 per share. This calculates to an annualized dividend of $5.68. The raised dividend is scheduled to be paid out on November 12, 2025, to shareholders of record as of October.

data.title: Innocan Pharma partners with animal sanctuary for injured animals, offering LPT-CBD remedy; ticker symbols: CSE: INNO, FSE: IP4, OTCQB: INNPF.
data.description: Innocan Pharma Corporation, a leading company in the pharmaceutical and biotechnology sectors with trading symbols CSE: INNO, FSE: IP4, and OTCQB: INNPF, recently revealed its continued partnership with Freedom Farm. The collaboration, based in Herzliya, Israel and Calgary, AB, began on September 18, 2025.

data.title: Performance Brokerage Services advised on the sale of McGrath Dubuque Harley-Davidson in Iowa from McGrath family to Jennerjohn and Meier.
data.description: Performance Brokerage Services, a prominent player in dealership transactions, has revealed the recent sale of McGrath Dubuque Harley-Davidson in Dubuque, Iowa. The dealership was sold from Mike McGrath and Pat McGrath to John Jennerjohn and Brody Meier. The transaction took place in Dubuque, Iowa, and attracted significant attention in the industry. The deal, finalized on Sept. 18, 2025, marks a noteworthy shift in ownership for the Harley-Davidson dealership. Performance Brokerage Services facilitated the sale, securing a successful transition of ownership between the involved parties.

data.title: Monster Energy hosts SMX Final Fueled by Monster in Las Vegas on Sept 19-20. Athletes, activations, & Monster Energy Compound featured.
data.description: The SMX Final Fueled by Monster is set to be held on September 19-20 in Las Vegas, marking a major event in the season. Athletes and activations will be the main focus at the Monster Energy Compound during this highly anticipated showdown. Las Vegas will witness the convergence of athletes gearing up for this significant event, all set to showcase their skills and compete for top honors. The event promises thrilling action and high-energy performances from participants, creating an engaging atmosphere for spectators. Stay tuned for an unforgettable experience as the event unfolds with numerous athletes vying for victory in this adrenaline-fueled competition.

data.title: Teamsters Local 210 members end 2-week strike at Perrigo's Bronx facility, ratifying new 3-year contract.
data.description: Members of Teamsters Local 210 in the Bronx, New York, have voted in favor of a new three-year contract after being on strike for over two weeks. The workers at Perrigo's production facility will return to work on Monday following the overwhelming approval of the deal. The exact number of workers who voted in favor of the contract was not disclosed.

data.title: MG Properties buys The Pearl at St. Rose for $64M, expanding Las Vegas presence in premier property deal.
data.description: In a recent announcement on September 18, 2025, MG Properties, a renowned real estate investment and management firm, revealed their successful acquisition of The Pearl at St. Rose for a substantial $64 million. This premium garden-style property is situated in the sought-after Silverado Ranch master-planned community in San Diego.

data.title: Everlight Solar wins Best of Madison 2025 award in Solar Energy category, reflecting community support.
data.description: In Verona, Wisconsin on September 18, 2025, Everlight Solar was recognized as the Best of Madison 2025 in the Solar Energy category by Madison Magazine readers. This award highlights the company's dedication to solar energy and outstanding service, showcasing the strong support it receives from the community.

data.title: PVAMU introduces Presidential Executive-in-Residence Program with Dr. Mathew Knowles. Connects students/faculty with industry leaders. Initiated in 2025.
data.description: In Prairie View, Texas on September 18, 2025, Prairie View A&M University revealed the launch of their Presidential Executive-in-Residence Program. This program introduces students and faculty to renowned industry leaders to share insights from their successful careers. It aims to provide valuable networking opportunities and real-world perspectives.

data.title: ACTFL recognizes outstanding contributions in language education with annual awards in New Orleans on Nov. Numbers undisclosed.
data.description: In Alexandria, Virginia, on September 18, 2025, ACTFL unveiled the winners of the 2025 ACTFL Professional Awards. This prestigious recognition honors individuals making remarkable contributions to language education. The awards ceremony is scheduled to take place in New Orleans on Friday, November...

data.title: Jasper Therapeutics plans a public offering of common stock to fund development of antibody therapy; total proceeds TBD.
data.description: Jasper Therapeutics, Inc., a biotech company, plans to launch a public offering to sell shares of its common stock or pre-funded warrants, targeting to raise funds for the development of briquilimab, an antibody therapy for mast cell driven diseases like chronic spontaneous urticaria (CSU), chronic inducible urticaria (CIndU), and asthma. The proceeds will be used for advancing its preclinical and clinical programs for briquilimab and for general corporate purposes, including capital expenditures and administrative expenses. The offering is subject to market conditions, and the exact size or completion date is uncertain. No specific numbers related to the offer size or financial goals were provided in the article.

data.title: Precore Gold closes first tranche of private placement, raising C$1,000,029.60 with 5,555,720 units sold at C$0.18 per unit.
data.description: Precore Gold Corp. (CSE: PRCG) based in Vancouver, British Columbia, reported the completion of the initial tranche of its non-brokered private placement, as of September 18, 2025. The private placement, initially announced on August 21, 2025, yielded total gross proceeds of C$1,000,029.60. This phase involved the sale of 5,555,720 units at a price of C$0.18 per unit, each unit comprising one common share and one-half of a common share purchase warrant. The warrant entitles the holder to acquire one additional common share for C$0.25 per warrant share within a thirty-six-month period.

data.title: Historic soccer showdown: Argentina vs Puerto Rico at Soldier Field on October 13, 2025, capacity 61,500. Part of AFA U.S. Friendly Tour.
data.description: Chicago, (BUSINESS WIRE) - VMG Sports & Entertainment, in partnership with the Argentine Football Association (AFA) and the Puerto Rican Football Federation (FPF), is excited to announce a unique international friendly match: Argentina vs Puerto Rico. The match is set to take place on Monday, October 13, 2025, at Soldier Field in Chicago at 7 PM CT. Soldier Field has a capacity of 61,500. This event is a part of the AFA U.S. Friendly Tour leading up to the 2026 FIFA World Cup, which kicks off with Argentina facing off against Venezuela in Miami.

data.title: TransAlta (TSX: TA) to host Investor Day in Toronto on Nov 18, 2025 from 9:00 am - 11:30 am (ET).
data.description: An upcoming Investor Day by TransAlta Corporation, a leading company in the sector (TSX: TA) (NYSE: TAC), will take place in Toronto on November 18, 2025. The event is scheduled to start at 9:00 a.m. (ET) (7:00 a.m. MT) and is anticipated to conclude by about 11:30 a.m. (ET) (9:30 a.m. MT).

data.title: Gevo partners with Biorecro for carbon credit deal, boosting revenue stream and expanding business with an in-operation project.
data.description: Gevo's carbon division is growing, providing a dependable revenue stream for the company while enhancing Biorecro's BECCS project lineup with a new project in operation. This expansion signifies a significant increase in revenue sources for Gevo and highlights the success of Biorecro's BECCS initiatives.

data.title: Scholastic reports fiscal Q1 2026 results, $225.6M revenue, $92.2M operating loss, and $55.7M adjusted EBITDA; focus on growing strategic initiatives.
data.description: Scholastic Corporation reported its financial results for the fiscal first quarter ended August 31, 2025. The company experienced a seasonal operating loss of $92.2 million, which was expected due to schools not being in session during the summer. Despite this, Scholastic made progress in strategic initiatives, particularly in the Children's Book Group segment with encouraging fall book fair bookings. Adjusted EBITDA improved by 8% to a loss of $55.7 million. Revenues decreased by 5% to $225.6 million, with the Education Solutions segment experiencing a 28% decline due to funding uncertainties. The Children's Book Publishing and Distribution segment saw a 4% increase in revenues to $109.4 million. The Entertainment segment revenues decreased by 18% to $13.6 million, and International revenues increased by 4% to $59.4 million. The company is affirming its fiscal 2026 guidance and remains focused on strengthening its balance sheet for long-term growth and impact. Scholastic retained Newmark Group for potential sale-leaseback transactions to monetize its real estate assets. The company had a net cash use of $100.2 million and net debt of $242.8 million in the first quarter. They distributed $5.2 million in dividends and plan to continue purchasing shares for the foreseeable future. Investors can access additional financial information through non-GAAP calculations and presentations. CEO Peter Warwick and CFO Haji Glover will discuss the results in a conference call on September 18, 2025.
